semantic.role: Infrastructure factory implementing the Factory Method pattern to instantiate provider-specific LLM transport clients. Acts as the central point for provider selection and initialization in the ArchSpine LLM subsystem.
responsibilities: Map provider name strings (e.g., 'openai', 'deepseek', 'gemini') to corresponding concrete LLM client implementations (OpenAICompatibleClient, GeminiClient).
Construct and return an LLMTransport instance configured with the given ProviderConfig, abstracting away provider-specific instantiation details.
Perform case-insensitive provider name matching to ensure robust selection.
Throw an Error for unsupported provider names to enforce a closed set of known providers.
outOfScope: Handling network communication, request lifecycle, or token estimation – delegated to the transport clients.
Managing provider API keys or credential rotation – expected from ProviderConfig.
Performing prompt formatting or inference logic.
Provider-specific configuration validation beyond what is required for instantiation.
invariants: The createTransport method must always return an object conforming to the LLMTransport interface.
Provider name matching must be case-insensitive (via toLowerCase).
Unsupported provider names must throw an Error.
The factory must not leak provider-specific imports or implementation details to consumers.

changeIntent.architecturalIntent: Provide a stable, single-entry point for LLM transport creation that isolates provider instantiation logic and allows easy addition of new providers without affecting consumers.
changeIntent.recentChangeIntent: The git log indicates recent fixes for ESLint errors, E2E prompt injection, and better-sqlite3 compatibility. These are maintenance and compatibility fixes with no behavioral change to the factory itself (e.g., no new providers or signature changes).

publicSurface.description: Factory class providing a static createTransport method for constructing LLM transport clients. This is the sole exported symbol from the module, consumed by higher-level orchestration layers to obtain a configured LLMTransport for a given provider name.
